VAV: Variable Air Volume box - a terminal unit that controls airflow to a space. At P&J typically includes reheat coil for perimeter zones.
VAV Box with Reheat: A Variable Air Volume terminal unit with an integrated heating coil. Used in perimeter zones to offset envelope heat loss. Typically hot water reheat.
AHU: Air Handling Unit - central equipment that conditions and distributes air throughout a building. Includes filters, coils, fans, and dampers.
FCU: Fan Coil Unit - a small terminal unit with a fan and coil. Used in perimeter zones or individual rooms.
SMACNA: Sheet Metal and Air Conditioning Contractors National Association. Sets standards for ductwork fabrication, installation, and testing.
ASHRAE 62.1: ASHRAE standard for ventilation and indoor air quality. Defines minimum outdoor air requirements for occupied spaces.
ASHRAE 90.1: ASHRAE energy efficiency standard for buildings. Defines minimum performance requirements for HVAC, lighting, and envelope.
CHWS: Chilled Water Supply - the supply side of a chilled water system. Typically 44-45 degrees F supply temperature.
CHWR: Chilled Water Return - the return side of a chilled water system. Typically 54-55 degrees F return temperature.
HWS: Hot Water Supply - the supply side of a hydronic heating system. Typically 180 degrees F for high temp, 120-140 degrees F for low temp.
HWR: Hot Water Return - the return side of a hydronic heating system.
BAS: Building Automation System - the control system that monitors and controls HVAC, lighting, and other building systems.
BMS: Building Management System - same as BAS, used interchangeably.
VFD: Variable Frequency Drive - an electronic device that controls motor speed by varying the frequency of the electrical supply. Used on fans and pumps for energy savings.
DOAS: Dedicated Outdoor Air System - an air handling unit that provides only outdoor air for ventilation, separate from the primary HVAC system.
ERV: Energy Recovery Ventilator - a device that recovers heat and moisture from exhaust air to precondition incoming outdoor air.
Potable Water Backflow: Prevention of contaminated water from flowing back into the potable water supply. Requires backflow preventers at connection points.
RPZ: Reduced Pressure Zone backflow preventer - a mechanical device that prevents backflow in high hazard applications such as chemical feed systems.
MERV: Minimum Efficiency Reporting Value - a rating system for air filters. Higher MERV ratings indicate finer filtration. MERV 13 is common for commercial HVAC.
Hydronic System: A heating or cooling system that uses water as the heat transfer medium. Includes piping, pumps, coils, and terminal units.
PSIG: Pounds per Square Inch Gauge - a unit of pressure measurement relative to atmospheric pressure.
BTU: British Thermal Unit - a unit of heat energy. One BTU is the energy needed to raise one pound of water by one degree Fahrenheit.
CFM: Cubic Feet per Minute - a unit of airflow measurement.
GPM: Gallons per Minute - a unit of water flow measurement.
RFI: Request for Information - a formal question submitted by a contractor to the design team requesting clarification on project documents.
Submittal: A document or sample submitted by a contractor for design team review and approval before installation.
NFPA 13: National Fire Protection Association standard for the installation of sprinkler systems.
P&ID: Piping and Instrumentation Diagram - a detailed diagram showing piping, instrumentation, and control systems.
DX: Direct Expansion - a refrigeration system where refrigerant directly cools the air or water, as opposed to a chilled water system.
CCF: Contract Change Form - a document used to formally modify the scope, cost, or schedule of a construction contract.
OSC: Owner Supplemental Condition - a document issued by the owner to clarify or modify contract requirements.
Supplemental Instructions: Instructions issued by the architect or engineer to clarify the contract documents without changing the contract sum or time.
ACC: Autodesk Construction Cloud - a project management platform used by contractors for document management, RFIs, submittals, and field coordination.
Procore: A cloud-based construction management platform used for project management, document control, RFIs, submittals, and financial management.
Fieldwire: A field management platform used for task management, drawing markup, and punch lists on construction projects.
AS400: IBM AS/400 - an enterprise business system used by P&J for job costing, payroll, purchase orders, accounts payable, and financial reporting.
GMP: Guaranteed Maximum Price - a contract type where the contractor agrees to complete the project for a set maximum price.
SWPPP: Stormwater Pollution Prevention Plan - a document required by law that outlines measures to prevent stormwater pollution on a construction site.
IFC: Issued for Construction - the final set of drawings and specifications released for actual construction.
CCO: Contract Change Order - a formal amendment to the construction contract that modifies scope, cost, or schedule.
NTP: Notice to Proceed - a written authorization from the owner to the contractor to begin work.
CO: Change Order - a written amendment to the construction contract modifying scope, price, or schedule.
LOI: Letter of Intent - a document indicating intent to award a contract before the formal contract is executed.
AIA: American Institute of Architects - sets standard contract documents widely used in construction.
ASI: Architect Supplemental Instruction - a minor change issued by the architect that does not affect contract sum or time.
